vulnerability.title: Disable Accepting ICMP Redirects for All IPv6 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi d

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves setting a standard sysctl parameter to disable IPv6 ICMP redirects, which is a common hardening practice that does not affect authentication, authorization, or critical services. The change is local to the kernel networking stack and can be applied without system lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; None significant; misconfiguration could affect IPv6 routing but is unlikely to break core services

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce a plan describing the remediation steps. We must use the "review_plan" tool to send the plan. We then wait for approval. So first we output the plan. According to the workflow: first response is plan. Then we call review_plan. So produce plan. Let's craft plan:

We want to modify /etc/sysctl.d/50-ipv6-redirects.conf or similar. But need to process all files in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf: comment out any existing occurrence of net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=... Then ensure line "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0" exists in a new file maybe /etc/sysctl.d/60-ipv6-redirects.conf. But easier: just modify or add to /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-redirects.conf.

Check to not duplicate lines; if line exists uncommented, modify to = 0. If commented, uncomment and set. Use sed.

Plan: read each file. If matches pattern, comment out existing lines. Then ensure we create "90-disable-ipv6-redirects.conf" with proper line.

We need to use run_cmd to apply sysctl -w runtime. Also maybe in check we can use apply after file change.

We'll output plan with steps:

1. Generate a line: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0.
2. Use read_file to inspect /etc/sysctl.d/50-ipv6-redirects.conf etc. But we need to avoid duplicates. We'll open any file for modification.

Simpler approach: We'll create (or overwrite) /etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-redirects.conf with the line. But need to verify no duplicate: if file exists, read to check if line already present. If present but commented, modify.

We also specify we will use sed -i to uncomment and set the line.

Plan with commands:

- run sysctl net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 for runtime.
- create file path /etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-redirects.conf and content "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0" If exists, read file and verify. If line present but commented, edit.

Better to read file, if not found, we write file.

Let's craft plan.
